text:
Once upon a time, there was an old lady named Ethel. One spring morning, she decided to go for a walk down to the lake where the water sparklingly reflected the azure sky. As she strolled along the edge of the pond, picking fresh wildflowers to wear in her pocket, she heard a soft, joyful sing-song coming from the tall old poplar trees by the water's edge.

Following the melody, she discovered a group of colorful warblers flittering from branch to branch. They sang of springtime joys—birds nesting, flowers unfurling, and the laughter of children playing. Ethel knew these birds were a rarity in her town. She hurried home, eager to share what she had heard.

That evening, while she sat by the fireplace knitting, she couldn't shake the song from her head. With determination, she decided to learn more about this mysterious birdsong. That evening, under the flickering shadow of the fireplace, she googled the song
